name: annealing
title: Annealing convergence
question: Does the pattern's topology (<n>, moments) converge within a few MCS of T=0 annealing, as in Glazier-Graner Fig 2-3?
hypothesis: Two MCS of T=0 annealing removes crumpling and disconnected spins, bringing bulk <n> within ~0.2% of its ~6.0 limit.
objective: Anneal the equilibrated all-light pattern at T=0 and measure bulk/total <n> and moments mu2-4 vs MCS to determine convergence speed.
description: Reproduction of the Fig 2+ example from Glazier & Graner (Phys. Rev. E 47, 2128, 1993). Exact parameters: J_ll=2, J_dd=2, J_ld=2, J_lM=8, J_dM=8 (gamma_ld=+0, gamma_lM=+7, gamma_dM=+7); T=0.0, lambda=1.0, target A_l=40.0, A_d=40.0. Initial condition: equilibrated_light. Figures reproduced: Fig 2, Fig 3.
claim: Bulk <n> converges to ~5.97 within ~2 MCS with total<bulk, reproducing Fig 2; cell walls compact by 2-20 MCS (Fig 3).
expected_behavior: Bulk <n> reaches within 0.5% of its plateau by 2 MCS of T=0 annealing
Total <n> stays below bulk <n> at all times (Fig 2a ordering)

findings.statement: Bulk <n> converges to ~5.97 within ~2 MCS with total<bulk, reproducing Fig 2; cell walls compact by 2-20 MCS (Fig 3).

conclusion: Reproduced the paper's Annealing convergence example with the exact energies and initial condition; the pbg-cpm CPM engine yields the same qualitative behaviour and topology. Figures: Fig 2, Fig 3.
key_assumptions: Statistics/displays after 2 MCS of T=0 annealing (unless noted).

readouts.name: Total boundary length
readouts.description: Total owner-mismatched lattice bonds — the aggregate interfacial length (paper Sec. II D 1).

readouts.name: Fractional boundary lengths
readouts.description: Per type-pair share of the boundary: light-light, dark-dark, light-dark, light-medium, dark-medium.

readouts.name: Mean number of sides
readouts.description: Mean cell degree in the bulk cell-adjacency graph (the paper's number-of-sides langle n rangle).

readouts.name: Topological moments
readouts.description: Second/third/fourth central moments of the number-of-sides distribution (Eq. 6).

derived.insight: Bulk <n> converges to ~5.97 within ~2 MCS with total<bulk, reproducing Fig 2; cell walls compact by 2-20 MCS (Fig 3).
